'''Tim Bergin''' (born 29 July 1985) is an Irish [[rugby league]] footballer who has played in the 2010s. He has played at representative level for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level in the [[RFL Championship|Co-operative Championship]] for the [[Sheffield Eagles]], and for the [[Gloucestershire All Golds]], as a {{rlp|wg}}.<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Ireland Internationals Move Closer to Contracts">{{cite web|url=http://rli.ie/article/195/Ireland-Internationals-Move-Closer-to-Contracts|title=Ireland Internationals Move Closer to Contracts|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=18 January 2010|accessdate=7 March 2010}}</ref><ref name="Sheffield Eagles Offer Contracts to Irish Internationals">{{cite web|url=http://rli.ie/article/197/Sheffield-Eagles-Offer-Contracts-to-Irish-Internationals|title=Sheffield Eagles Offer Contracts to Irish Internationals|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=27 January 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100209214033/http://rli.ie/article/197/Sheffield-Eagles-Offer-Contracts-to-Irish-Internationals|accessdate=7 March 2010|archive-date=9 February 2010}}</ref>

==Background==
Tim Bergin was born in [[Abbeyleix]], Republic of Ireland.

==Playing career==
In October 2009, Bergin became the first [[Laois Panthers]] player to be selected for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] when he was named in the squad for the [[2009 European Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ireland-name-european-cup-squad |title=Ireland name European Cup squad |website=Love Rugby League |date=8 October 2009 |access-date=1 March 2024}}</ref>

On 3 November 2011 The annual [[RLIF Awards]] dinner was held at the [[Tower of London]], and Bergin was named [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]]'s player of the year.<ref>{{cite news|title=Slater scoops player award|url=https://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15313/7289077/Slater-scoops-player-award|accessdate=5 November 2011|newspaper= [[Sky Sports]]|date=3 November 2011}}</ref>

On 6 October 2012, Bergin announced to his fans on Facebook that he was taking a break from the game to pursue other opportunities. Loverugbyleague.com announced on 21 January 2013 that Bergin has returned to the game and signed for University of Gloucestershire All Golds.<ref>{{cite news|title=All Golds land ex-Eagle Bergin|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/all-golds-land-ex-eagle-bergin|accessdate=22 January 2012}}</ref>
